sections: HCM-1.1 additive kind/schema registry boundary proof gate

repo_truth: Review the exact manifest over baseline 87bc3ab3e175aa1d6c058ac0ed57495989fe5451. Fresh Final Registry Review 1 returned five Required findings over subject sha256:021e7f5090079a8ecd2c69be69d9a66102336da6b28896f243358c8d6bedffac; the parent accepted and remediated all five within HCM-1.1. The new subject includes the immutable Review 1 dispatch and both proof walls. Current canonical product paths still use the fixed four-variant CanonicalArtifactKind and do not call the new registry. Derive the verdict only from live authority, replayed bytes, and read-only verification.
allowed_scope: Remain read-only and replay every sorted subject-manifest entry plus aggregate before review.

Review every engine implementation, stable-role asset, test/fixture, HCM-1.1 packet/status/proof byte, prior immutable review dispatch, the single crosswalk row, and bounded gate-evidence change in the manifest.
Read unchanged canonical authority, entry handoff/ledger, live Cargo metadata, and official dependency documentation only as needed to validate the subject.
Run read-only focused, workspace, package, dependency-feature, archive, handoff, Git, source, security, and semantic checks needed to evaluate the subject.
Return findings first through the built-in channel with exact file and line evidence.
non_goals: No edits, staging, commits, new dispatches, global handoffs, ledger writes, or user-task hops.

No HCM-1.2 or later-slice work and no canonical artifact, layout, setup, doctor, flow, compiler, CLI, SDK, Tauri, Substrate, profile, instance, intake, renderer, lifecycle, Projection, publication, or downstream adoption.
Do not infer missing first-party schemas, semantic-capability contracts, intake/renderer/lifecycle/Projection definitions, shipped profile assets, or product authority.
Do not accept unit proof as product-path adoption or close PG-KIND-01/PG-KIND-02.
Do not require stylistic alternatives without a violated authority, invariant, security boundary, proof gate, or concrete defect.
tasks: Invoke the required skills, remain read-only, replay the exact sorted manifest and aggregate, and return BLOCKED if any subject byte differs.

Review findings first in Critical, Required, Optional, Nit order. Each actionable finding must include severity, exact file/line, violated contract or invariant, why the boundary/proof is wrong, smallest valid remediation, and missing verification.

Verify all five accepted Review 1 findings are fully remediated: every same-document/mixed reference edge counts toward one 32-edge depth/cycle model; every source component is opened descriptor-relative with no-follow semantics and the final handle is retained; loader reads are bounded before retention; typed errors redact absolute paths and refused reference content; and every previously missing literal proof case is now exercised.

Audit the Unix rustix 1.1.4 fs dependency repair, target configuration, lockfile edge, component/final open flags, retained-handle regular-file check, deterministic substitution test, and fail-closed non-Unix posture for containment, races, portability claims, errors, and unintended capability.

Verify the implementation matches the exact additive HCM-1.1 public owner boundary, exact source/asset topology, dependency pins/features, error contract, source budgets, TDD sequencing, proof wall, classification ceiling, and non-goals.

Audit exact-definition identity and fingerprint code for canonical SemVer, bounded lowercase-ASCII grammar, duplicate-key rejection, RFC 8785/SHA-256 correctness, limits, closed decoding, stable errors, and no machine-path/source-content disclosure.

Audit stable-role registry bytes and loaders for exact frozen fingerprint replay, package ownership, exact selection, closed nested types, role/category/duplicate/substitution refusal, bounded reads, aggregate accounting, and reusable descriptor-relative no-follow source access.

Audit schema registry prewalk and validator construction for exact Draft 2020-12, schema-aware keyword traversal, local JSON-Pointer-only refs, normalized allowed-root containment, descriptor-relative component/final no-follow access, exact internal resource identity, deterministic closure/fingerprint/location behavior, bounds/cycles/aliases, and total remote/ambient/dynamic/executable refusal.

Audit jsonschema configuration and the resolved dependency graph for disabled ambient resolver behavior, in-memory-only resources, linear regex, and absence of HTTP/TLS/async/file/custom execution paths.

Audit artifact-kind typed decoding, exact stable-role/schema dependency resolution, capability-free fingerprint closure, unordered role normalization, duplicate/conflict/source-order behavior, and refusal before fingerprinting of every later-owned, wrong-record, instance, setup, intake, opaque, forged, changed-byte, wrong-kind, or incompatible binding-shaped input.

Verify the public API has exact-ref lookups and deterministic structural validation without an untyped dependency dispatcher, and identify any fail-open, panic, misleading error, unbounded input, nondeterminism, ownership leak, or secret/path disclosure.

Verify the two-entry/two-kind custom fixture produces literal forward/reverse equality for registry fingerprints, exact lookup sets, closure members, both entry fingerprints/closures, both kind fingerprints, and valid/invalid behavior without a new product enum variant or command.

Replay or inspect every positive, negative, security, engine/workspace, feature-tree, package-content, archive, handoff, diff/scope, and GitNexus assertion in both proof walls; distinguish the superseded pre-review claims from the post-remediation proof and identify any asserted case not actually exercised or any required proof absent from the manifest.

Verify the crosswalk promotes only the additive owner-library seam to BoundaryLanded, PG-KIND-01 and PG-KIND-02 remain open with exact missing lifecycle/Projection and supplied-intake coverage, and no sibling gate or product-path claim is widened.
Return CLEAN only if no valid Critical or Required finding remains; otherwise return CHANGES_REQUIRED or BLOCKED. Make no edits.
stop_conditions: Return BLOCKED if the manifest, aggregate, entry authority, or required read-only verification cannot be reproduced.
Return CHANGES_REQUIRED rather than resolving a missing product, semantic-capability, lifecycle, intake, renderer, Projection, publication, or downstream decision by inference.
Return to the parent if remediation would require HCM-1.2+, a current product path, a sibling crate, or authority outside the exact HCM-1.1 packet.
